subject: Broken swing set at Stoney Creek Park
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I wanted to bring to your attention that the swing set on the east side of Stoney Creek Park has two broken chains — both on the middle swing. My kids and three other families discovered this Tuesday morning. One of the chain ends is hanging loose and caught my daughter's arm, thankfully just a scrape. It's been this way for at least a week based on what neighbors are saying. Please get someone out there before a child gets seriously hurt.

subject: Broken swing set at Stoney Creek Park
body_clean: Ms. Szymanski, thank you for flagging this right away — your daughter's safety is exactly why we want these reports promptly. I've already put in a work order with our parks maintenance crew; the swing set will be taken out of service this afternoon with caution tape until repairs are completed. We're targeting a full fix by end of week, pending part availability. I'll follow up directly once it's back in service.

subject: Broken swing set at Stoney Creek Park
body_clean: Kevin, I really appreciate the fast response. I drove by this afternoon and saw the caution tape — thank you for doing that so quickly. Looking forward to hearing when it's fixed. It's a popular spot all summer and the kids really miss it when equipment is down. Thanks again to you and the Mayor's office.

subject: Summer concert series — can we get more dates?
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, my family came out to the first two concerts at Douglas Park this summer and honestly it's been the highlight of our month. The jazz set on June 14th especially — the turnout was massive. Is there any possibility of adding a couple more dates before Labor Day? Even a smaller acoustic setup would do. There's clearly an appetite for it in the community and it brings people out in a really positive way.

subject: Summer concert series — can we get more dates?
body_clean: Marcus, thank you — hearing that feedback about the jazz night genuinely made my day. Kevin O'Brien in our Parks office is already looking at the budget to see whether we can squeeze in one or two more events before September. No promises yet, but your message is going directly to him. Keep an eye on the Village website and our Facebook page for any announcements. Appreciate you coming out and being part of what makes Bellwood great.

subject: Taste of Bellwood was wonderful this year
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I just wanted to drop a quick note to say the Taste of Bellwood this year was truly outstanding. The food vendors were top-notch, the stage lineup on Saturday evening kept my whole extended family dancing, and the street cleanup crew was working all night — things looked pristine Sunday morning. My mother, who has lived on Bohland Ave for 41 years, said it was the best one she can remember. Please pass my thanks to everyone involved.

subject: Youth basketball clinic — any plans this summer?
body_clean: Hi, I'm a dad with two boys ages 10 and 13 who live on Harvard Ave. Last year I heard something about a youth basketball clinic in the park but by the time I found out it was already full. Is there anything planned for this summer? If so, where do we sign up? These kids need structured activity and the park courts are the only place they've got. Would really appreciate more advance notice this year if something's happening.

subject: Youth basketball clinic — any plans this summer?
body_clean: Mr. Hutchins, great to hear from you. We are running two sessions of the youth basketball clinic this summer — Session A begins July 21st and Session B starts August 11th, both at the courts behind Village Hall on Washington Blvd. Registration opened this Monday on the Village website; sessions are free and limited to 24 kids each. Your boys sound like exactly the age group we're targeting. I'll also make sure we push the announcement more broadly this year — I hear you on the late notice last summer, and that was on us.

subject: Trash overflowing at Mannheim Park during concerts
body_clean: Mayor, I love the summer concert series but I need to say something about the garbage situation. Last Friday there were only two small bins for what looked like 200-plus people and by 7 pm they were overflowing. Cups and wrappers were blowing into the street and onto neighboring yards. Can the Village either increase trash pickup frequency during events or bring in extra receptacles? It's an easy fix and it would make a real difference in how the neighborhood feels about hosting these events.

subject: Trash overflowing at Mannheim Park during concerts
body_clean: Ms. Fogarty, this is completely fair feedback and I'm embarrassed it slipped through our planning. We underestimated attendance at the last two events significantly. For all remaining concerts this season I'm arranging six additional 32-gallon containers placed along the perimeter and a mid-event pickup around 7:30 pm. I've also looped in Public Works to make sure the area gets swept first thing Saturday mornings. Thank you for writing in rather than just posting on social media — that kind of direct communication helps us fix things faster.

subject: Thank you for the Taste of Bellwood
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, just a heartfelt thank you for the Taste of Bellwood last weekend. I've been coming since I moved to Eastern Ave eight years ago and this year felt extra special. The live gospel set Sunday afternoon gave me chills. My nephew who was visiting from Indianapolis said he's thinking about moving here. That's the kind of event that builds community pride. Thank you and your whole team for pulling it together every year.

subject: Merry-go-round at 25th Ave tot lot is a hazard
body_clean: Mayor, the spinning merry-go-round at the small playground on 25th Ave near Monroe St has a rusted handle bar and the platform edge has lifted on one side creating a lip that kids can catch a foot on. My wife and I bring our grandchildren there every Sunday morning and I've been putting a piece of cardboard over that edge as a temporary fix, which is ridiculous. This needs a proper repair. The equipment looks like it hasn't been assessed in years.

subject: Request for girls soccer camp or programming
body_clean: Hello Mayor Okonkwo. I'm writing on behalf of several moms in the neighborhood who would love to see a girls soccer program offered through the Village this summer. My daughter is nine and has been asking to play for two years but private leagues are expensive and far away. We have open fields behind the library. Even a 4-week Saturday morning camp with volunteer coaches would be a start. Other kids deserve the same opportunities as families with more resources. Would you be open to a conversation about this?

subject: Request for girls soccer camp or programming
body_clean: Aaliyah, thank you for writing and for organizing other parents around this — that kind of grassroots energy is exactly what helps us build programming. I agree completely that cost shouldn't be a barrier. I'm connecting you with Kevin O'Brien in Parks who is actively developing our fall youth sports calendar. A summer pilot is a tighter lift given where we are in the season, but a fall session starting in September is very realistic. Kevin will reach out to you this week. Please don't let the summer window discourage you — this conversation is already moving.

subject: Request for girls soccer camp or programming
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, thank you so much for the quick reply and for taking this seriously. Fall would be great — honestly we'd love anything. I'll wait to hear from Kevin and in the meantime I'll keep gathering names of interested families. We can easily put together a list of 20 or 30 kids if it helps make the case. Really appreciate you being so responsive.

subject: Great job at Taste of Bellwood — vendors were excellent
body_clean: Mayor, just wanted to say the Taste of Bellwood was a home run this year. The rib vendor near the main stage was phenomenal and the DJ set between bands kept the energy going instead of dead air like we've had before. My whole block on Granville Ave came out as a group for the first time. One small note: parking on Frederick Ave got chaotic around 6 pm. Maybe a couple of cones or a volunteer directing traffic would help next year. Overall though, really well done.

subject: Shade structures needed at the playground on 50th Ave
body_clean: Good morning Mayor Okonkwo. I take my toddler to the playground at 50th Ave nearly every morning in summer and the heat coming off the metal equipment by 10 am is genuinely dangerous — the slide surface last Tuesday was hot enough that my son cried. I know a full shade structure is a capital expense, but even a sail shade or two over the slide and climbing frame would make a huge difference. Other towns have done these inexpensively. I'd be happy to help research grants if that would move things along.

subject: Shade structures needed at the playground on 50th Ave
body_clean: Ms. Sandoval, thank you for writing and for the very practical offer to help with grant research — I want to take you up on that. We've had shade structures on our wish list for two playgrounds for a couple of years now. The OSLAD grant through the Illinois DNR is one avenue we haven't fully pursued and a matching private effort could strengthen our application. I'm going to set up a call with you in the next two weeks. In the meantime I'll check whether we have any portable canopy inventory we can deploy at 50th Ave this season as a stopgap.

subject: Concert volume too loud for nearby residents
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I live on Bellwood Ave about a block from where the summer concerts are held and I want to raise a concern respectfully. I support the concerts — they're a nice idea. But last Thursday the bass from the speakers was audible inside my house with the windows shut until nearly 9:30 pm. I'm a light sleeper and so is my elderly mother who lives with me. Is there any way to cap the volume or shift the start time earlier so things wrap up by 8:45? I don't want to be the neighbor who ruins it for everyone, but I need to be able to sleep.

subject: Concert volume too loud for nearby residents
body_clean: Ms. Dunning, thank you for reaching out and for raising this so thoughtfully. The Mayor asked me to respond directly. Your concern is completely valid — we want the concerts to be an asset to the neighborhood, not a disruption to residents who live closest to them. I've flagged this for Kevin O'Brien in Parks and for our sound vendor contact. Going forward, we will ask the sound technician to reduce low-frequency output after 8 pm and the program will end no later than 8:45 pm for remaining events this season. I hope that gives you and your mother some much-needed rest.

subject: Can we get a portable water fountain at Douglas Park this summer?
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I coach a youth flag football team that practices at Douglas Park on Tuesday and Thursday evenings. The nearest water fountain is almost a quarter mile away near the parking lot and the kids — ages 8 to 12 — are draining water bottles in the first 20 minutes on hot days. I've seen portable drinking fountains that attach to a fire hydrant or garden hose. Is there any way the Village could make one available for summer evening practices? Even a temporary solution would be a tremendous help for our kids' safety.

subject: Kids loved the summer reading kickoff at the park
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I just want to say what a wonderful event the library's summer reading kickoff was at the park on Saturday. My three kids were completely engaged — the storytelling tent, the craft activities, the little scavenger hunt through the park. It was free, it was educational, and the staff were so kind and patient. I live on Morris Ave and we walked over. Events like this make me proud to live in Bellwood. Please keep investing in programming like this.
